definition: A performance metric used to evaluate the profitability of an investment by comparing the gain or loss relative to its cost.
extendedDefinition: ROI is calculated as net profit divided by the cost of investment, typically expressed as a percentage, making it straightforward to compare the efficiency of different investments. It is one of the most widely used financial metrics across business functions, from marketing campaigns and capital expenditures to software purchases and training programs. While simple on its face, effective ROI analysis requires careful attribution of both costs and returns, which can be challenging for indirect or long-term investments. Understanding ROI helps stakeholders justify budgets, prioritize initiatives, and measure the tangible value of their decisions.
examples: Calculating the ROI of a CRM implementation by tracking sales productivity gains
Measuring marketing campaign ROI by comparing revenue generated against ad spend
SaaS tool ROI evaluation comparing subscription costs against time savings
description: Return on Investment (ROI) measures the profitability of an investment relative to its cost. Learn how to calculate and maximize software ROI.
